name: dbt Labs
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of dbt Labs' design language — the analytics-engineering platform built on a deep navy-violet (#262a38) canvas, a signature dbt-orange (#ff694a) accent, and a hex-pixel motif (the 'dbt cube' / pixel-cluster mark) recurring across the surface. The brand reads as 'analytics engineering' — half data-warehouse-serious, half developer-tool-friendly, with the SQL editor as a recurring hero device.

design_language.motion_signature: 300ms ease-out on hover; signature is the dbt-pixel-cube mark with a subtle 4s rotation; SQL editor hero has a syntax-highlight type-in at ~30 chars/sec on first paint.

trademark_signals: dbt-orange (#ff694a) — the warm coral-orange accent unique to dbt Labs, used on CTAs, key display words, and the brand-mark pixel cluster
Pixel-cube / dbt-cube mark — an isometric 3D cube assembled from small orange-and-violet pixels — appears in hero illustrations and as the brand glyph
Dual-canvas system: cream (#fefcf7) for marketing surfaces, navy-violet (#262a38) for product surfaces and pre-footer CTAs — the dual mode is the brand's pacing rhythm
SQL editor as recurring hero — every product page shows a real dbt SQL/Jinja code block with syntax highlighting (orange keywords, violet macros, cream identifiers)
Secondary violet (#7a5af8) — used on the pixel-cube mark and as a hover/active state on the dark surfaces
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute the dbt-orange with a brand red or amber — the specific warm-coral #ff694a IS the brand
Don't drop the pixel-cube mark. The isometric assembled-from-pixels glyph IS the brand identity
Don't replace the SQL editor hero with an abstract illustration — the real dbt code IS the trust signal
Don't add a third accent. The brand is orange + violet (sub) + navy + cream — four colors, no more
Don't put the brand orange on a gradient. The orange is solid-fill only
